themes.hook: The West Asia war didn't just hit the oil markets. It hit your office lunch.
key_data_points: 20% rationing of commercial LPG for Indian restaurants and eateries implemented by OMCs.
8-10% projected hit to daily City Gas Distribution (CGD) sales volumes due to Middle East disruptions.
IT giants like Infosys and HCLTech advising employees to bring home-cooked food due to fuel shortages.
themes.non_obvious_angle: Energy security is no longer just a macro-economic stat for the Finance Ministry; it has become a human resources and work-from-home policy driver. When basic utility shortages force hybrid work shifts, the fragility of India's 'service superpower' status is exposed.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, how do you view the irony of India aiming for 'Sovereign AI' and 5G dominance while our IT hubs are physically constrained by the availability of cooking gas for their cafeterias?

source_articles.title: India's oil minister says starting today there will be LPG allocation to restaurants
Infosys, HCLTech, Cognizant advise employees to bring food amid LPG shortage
Middle East disruptions to impact daily CGD sales volume by 8-10%

themes.hook: India is finally tired of being the world's 'screwdriver' economy.
key_data_points: ₹6,200 crore investment by Waaree Energies into 10 GW of integrated solar ingot and wafer capacity.
TSMC’s N3 logic wafer capacity hitting a ceiling, creating a global 'logic' bottleneck.
US easing sanctions pressure on India to buy Russian crude, giving India strategic leverage as a 'swing consumer'.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The real story isn't 'Make in India'—it's 'Control the Bottleneck in India.' By moving from assembly to solar ingots and wafers (the upstream core), India is finally attacking the structural dependency on China that subsidies alone couldn't fix.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is this shift to deep manufacturing (wafers/ingots) a sign that India is finally moving past the low-margin 'assembly' phase of the PLI schemes?

source_articles.title: Waaree Energies to set up India’s largest 10 GW integrated solar ingot & wafer facility
TSMC's N3 logic wafer capacity has become one of the AI industry's biggest constraints
White House begging India to buy Russian crude amidst Iran war

themes.hook: Maharashtra is literally selling its teak forests to fund a monthly ₹1,500 cash transfer.
key_data_points: ₹1,500 per month promised to low-income women under the Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana.
Karnataka's growth engine is sputtering under a debt problem that is 'only getting worse'.
RBI injecting ₹50,000 crore liquidity via OMOs to manage tax-related cash crunches.
themes.non_obvious_angle: We are witnessing the 'Asset-to-Welfare' arbitrage. States are trading long-term environmental and capital assets (teak forests) to sustain short-term consumption liquidity. It’s a fiscal 'Pay Later' scheme where the interest is paid in ecological and structural degradation.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, as a finance professional, what's your take on the sustainability of using natural capital to fund recurring revenue expenditures at the state level?

source_articles.title: Maharashtra considering raising funds from teak forests to finance welfare scheme
Rich state, borrowed money: Karnataka’s growth story has a debt problem

themes.hook: Housing sales are down. Yet, the industry just made 16% more money than last year. Welcome to the K-shaped real estate market.
key_data_points: 3% decline in housing sales volume across 50 Indian cities.
16% surge in total sales value, reaching a staggering ₹8.4 lakh crore.
Only 6.14 lakh units sold despite the record-breaking capital inflow.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The Indian middle class is being priced out of their own dream by 'Margin Optimization.' Developers have realized they make more money building one luxury villa for a CXO than ten affordable apartments for his staff. The volume dip isn't a lack of demand; it's a lack of affordable supply.
themes.your_take_prompt: Does the divergence between sales volume and value suggest that the Indian real estate market is becoming an 'asset class for the few' rather than a 'utility for the many'?

source_articles.title: Housing sales in 50 cities dip 3% to 6.14 lakh units, up 16% in value to Rs 8.4 lakh crore

themes.hook: The US just put India on a 'forced labour' trade probe. Don't be fooled—this isn't just about human rights; it's about the new protectionism.
key_data_points: US Section 301 trade probe targets 16 economies, including India.
59 other nations included in the 'forced labour' trade watch list.
India holding off on signing the US trade deal for 'several months' due to tariff complexities.
themes.non_obvious_angle: ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) is being weaponized as a non-tariff barrier. When Western economies can't compete with Indian manufacturing on price, they pivot to 'Process Standards.' 'China Plus One' is only a reality if you play by the 'One's' specific rulebook.
themes.your_take_prompt: How should Indian exporters navigate this shift where 'compliance' is the new 'competitiveness'?

source_articles.title: US initiates ‘forced labour’ trade probe against India, 59 others
India To Hold Off On US Deal For Several Months
Why is Trump targeting 16 economies, including India? Section 301
